summary_zh: 推理已经成为大型语言模型的一项关键能力。在语言任务中，可以通过自提升技术来增强这种能力，这些技术可以改进推理路径以供后续微调。然而，将这些基于语言的自提升方法扩展到视觉语言模型（VLM）面临着一个独特的挑战：推理路径中的视觉幻觉无法被有效验证或纠正。我们的解决方案始于对视觉对比的一个关键观察：当呈现一对对比性的VQA（视觉问答）对，即两张视觉上相似但问题同义的图像时，VLM可以更精确地识别相关的视觉线索。受到这一观察的启发，我们提出了视觉对比自授推理器（VC-STaR），这是一个新颖的自提升框架，它利用视觉对比来缓解模型生成的推理过程中的幻觉。我们收集了一套多样化的VQA数据集，根据多模态相似性策划对比性样本对，并使用VC-STaR生成推理过程。由此，我们获得了一个新的视觉推理数据集VisCoR-55K，然后通过监督微调来提升各种VLM的推理能力。大量的实验表明，VC-STaR不仅优于现有的自提升方法，而且超越了在SoTA视觉推理数据集上微调的模型，这表明VLM固有的对比能力可以引导它们进行自我视觉推理。项目地址：https://github.com/zhiyupan42/VC-STaR。

title: AnchorDrive: LLM Scenario Rollout with Anchor-Guided Diffusion Regeneration for Safety-Critical Scenario Generation
summary: Autonomous driving systems require comprehensive evaluation in safety-critical scenarios to ensure safety and robustness. However, such scenarios are rare and difficult to collect from real-world driving data, necessitating simulation-based synthesis. Yet, existing methods often exhibit limitations in both controllability and realism. From a capability perspective, LLMs excel at controllable generation guided by natural language instructions, while diffusion models are better suited for producing trajectories consistent with realistic driving distributions. Leveraging their complementary strengths, we propose AnchorDrive, a two-stage safety-critical scenario generation framework. In the first stage, we deploy an LLM as a driver agent within a closed-loop simulation, which reasons and iteratively outputs control commands under natural language constraints; a plan assessor reviews these commands and provides corrective feedback, enabling semantically controllable scenario generation. In the second stage, the LLM extracts key anchor points from the first-stage trajectories as guidance objectives, which jointly with other guidance terms steer the diffusion model to regenerate complete trajectories with improved realism while preserving user-specified intent. Experiments on the highD dataset demonstrate that AnchorDrive achieves superior overall performance in criticality, realism, and controllability, validating its effectiveness for generating controllable and realistic safety-critical scenarios.
tldr: The paper introduces AnchorDrive, a two-stage framework leveraging LLMs and diffusion models for generating controllable and realistic safety-critical driving scenarios. It uses an LLM in a closed-loop simulation for initial scenario generation and then employs a diffusion model guided by anchors extracted from the LLM's trajectories to enhance realism.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了AnchorDrive，这是一个利用LLM和扩散模型的两阶段框架，用于生成可控且逼真的安全关键驾驶场景。它在闭环仿真中使用LLM进行初始场景生成，然后使用由LLM轨迹提取的锚点引导扩散模型，以增强真实感。
summary_zh: 自动驾驶系统需要在安全关键场景中进行全面评估，以确保安全性和鲁棒性。然而，此类场景在真实驾驶数据中稀有且难以采集，因此需要基于仿真的合成。然而，现有方法通常在可控性和真实性方面都存在局限性。从能力角度来看，大型语言模型（LLM）擅长在自然语言指令指导下进行可控生成，而扩散模型更适合生成与真实驾驶分布一致的轨迹。利用它们的互补优势，我们提出AnchorDrive，一种两阶段安全关键场景生成框架。在第一阶段，我们在闭环仿真中部署一个LLM作为驾驶代理，该代理在自然语言约束下进行推理并迭代输出控制命令；一个计划评估器审查这些命令并提供修正反馈，从而实现语义可控的场景生成。在第二阶段，LLM从第一阶段的轨迹中提取关键锚点作为引导目标，这些锚点与其他引导项共同引导扩散模型重新生成具有改进真实性的完整轨迹，同时保留用户指定的意图。在highD数据集上的实验表明，AnchorDrive在关键性、真实性和可控性方面均实现了卓越的总体性能，验证了其生成可控和真实的安全性关键场景的有效性。

title: MemSifter: Offloading LLM Memory Retrieval via Outcome-Driven Proxy Reasoning
summary: As Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used for long-duration tasks, maintaining effective long-term memory has become a critical challenge. Current methods often face a trade-off between cost and accuracy. Simple storage methods often fail to retrieve relevant information, while complex indexing methods (such as memory graphs) require heavy computation and can cause information loss. Furthermore, relying on the working LLM to process all memories is computationally expensive and slow. To address these limitations, we propose MemSifter, a novel framework that offloads the memory retrieval process to a small-scale proxy model. Instead of increasing the burden on the primary working LLM, MemSifter uses a smaller model to reason about the task before retrieving the necessary information. This approach requires no heavy computation during the indexing phase and adds minimal overhead during inference. To optimize the proxy model, we introduce a memory-specific Reinforcement Learning (RL) training paradigm. We design a task-outcome-oriented reward based on the working LLM's actual performance in completing the task. The reward measures the actual contribution of retrieved memories by mutiple interactions with the working LLM, and discriminates retrieved rankings by stepped decreasing contributions. Additionally, we employ training techniques such as Curriculum Learning and Model Merging to improve performance. We evaluated MemSifter on eight LLM memory benchmarks, including Deep Research tasks. The results demonstrate that our method meets or exceeds the performance of existing state-of-the-art approaches in both retrieval accuracy and final task completion. MemSifter offers an efficient and scalable solution for long-term LLM memory. We have open-sourced the model weights, code, and training data to support further research.
tldr: MemSifter offloads LLM memory retrieval to a smaller proxy model trained with reinforcement learning, improving efficiency and accuracy on long-duration tasks.
tldr_zh: MemSifter将LLM内存检索卸载到一个较小的代理模型，该模型通过强化学习进行训练，从而提高了长时间任务的效率和准确性。
summary_zh: 随着大型语言模型（LLMs）越来越多地应用于长时间任务，保持有效的长期记忆已成为一项关键挑战。目前的方法通常面临成本和准确性之间的权衡。简单的存储方法往往无法检索到相关信息，而复杂的索引方法（例如记忆图）需要大量的计算，并可能导致信息丢失。此外，依赖工作中的LLM处理所有记忆在计算上既昂贵又缓慢。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了一种新型框架MemSifter，该框架将记忆检索过程卸载到小型代理模型。MemSifter不是增加主要工作LLM的负担，而是使用较小的模型在检索必要信息之前对任务进行推理。这种方法在索引阶段不需要大量的计算，并且在推理期间增加了最小的开销。为了优化代理模型，我们引入了一种特定于记忆的强化学习（RL）训练范式。我们设计了一种面向任务结果的奖励，该奖励基于工作LLM在完成任务中的实际表现。该奖励通过与工作LLM的多次交互来衡量检索到的记忆的实际贡献，并通过阶梯式递减贡献来区分检索到的排名。此外，我们采用课程学习和模型合并等训练技术来提高性能。我们在包括深度研究任务在内的八个LLM记忆基准上评估了MemSifter。结果表明，我们的方法在检索准确性和最终任务完成度方面均达到或超过了现有最先进方法的性能。MemSifter为长期LLM记忆提供了一种高效且可扩展的解决方案。我们已经开源了模型权重、代码和训练数据，以支持进一步的研究。

title: AOI: Turning Failed Trajectories into Training Signals for Autonomous Cloud Diagnosis
summary: Large language model (LLM) agents offer a promising data-driven approach to automating Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), yet their enterprise deployment is constrained by three challenges: restricted access to proprietary data, unsafe action execution under permission-governed environments, and the inability of closed systems to improve from failures. We present AOI (Autonomous Operations Intelligence), a trainable multi-agent framework formulating automated operations as a structured trajectory learning problem under security constraints. Our approach integrates three key components. First, a trainable diagnostic system applies Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to distill expert-level knowledge into locally deployed open-source models, enabling preference-based learning without exposing sensitive data. Second, a read-write separated execution architecture decomposes operational trajectories into observation, reasoning, and action phases, allowing safe learning while preventing unauthorized state mutation. Third, a Failure Trajectory Closed-Loop Evolver mines unsuccessful trajectories and converts them into corrective supervision signals, enabling continual data augmentation. Evaluated on the AIOpsLab benchmark, our contributions yield cumulative gains. (1) The AOI runtime alone achieves 66.3% best@5 success on all 86 tasks, outperforming the prior state-of-the-art (41.9%) by 24.4 points. (2) Adding Observer GRPO training, a locally deployed 14B model reaches 42.9% avg@1 on 63 held-out tasks with unseen fault types, surpassing Claude Sonnet 4.5. (3) The Evolver converts 37 failed trajectories into diagnostic guidance, improving end-to-end avg@5 by 4.8 points while reducing variance by 35%.
tldr: The paper introduces AOI, a trainable multi-agent framework using LLMs for autonomous cloud diagnosis. It addresses security concerns and improves performance through expert knowledge distillation, secure execution, and failure trajectory learning.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了AOI，一个使用LLM进行自主云诊断的可训练多智能体框架。它通过专家知识提取、安全执行和失败轨迹学习来解决安全问题并提高性能。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）代理为自动化站点可靠性工程（SRE）提供了一种有前景的数据驱动方法，但其企业部署受到三个挑战的制约：对专有数据的访问受限、在权限管理环境下不安全的操作执行，以及封闭系统无法从失败中改进。我们提出了AOI（自主运维智能），一个可训练的多代理框架，将自动化运维构建为安全约束下的结构化轨迹学习问题。我们的方法集成了三个关键组件。首先，一个可训练的诊断系统应用组相对策略优化（GRPO）将专家级知识提炼到本地部署的开源模型中，从而在不暴露敏感数据的情况下实现基于偏好的学习。其次，一个读写分离的执行架构将运维轨迹分解为观察、推理和行动阶段，在防止未授权状态变更的同时实现安全学习。第三，一个失败轨迹闭环进化器挖掘不成功的轨迹，并将它们转换为纠正性监督信号，从而实现持续的数据增强。在AIOpsLab基准测试中，我们的贡献产生了累积增益。（1）仅AOI运行时就在所有86个任务上实现了66.3%的best@5成功率，超过了先前的最佳水平（41.9%）24.4个百分点。（2）添加观察者GRPO训练后，本地部署的14B模型在63个具有未见过的故障类型的保留任务上达到了42.9%的avg@1，超过了Claude Sonnet 4.5。（3）进化器将37个失败的轨迹转换为诊断指导，将端到端avg@5提高了4.8个百分点，同时将方差降低了35%。

title: Tool Verification for Test-Time Reinforcement Learning
summary: Test-time reinforcement learning (TTRL) has emerged as a promising paradigm for self-evolving large reasoning models (LRMs), enabling online adaptation on unlabeled test inputs via self-induced rewards through majority voting. However, a spurious yet high-frequency unverified consensus can become a biased and reinforced reward signal, leading to incorrect mode collapse. We address this failure mode with T^3RL (Tool-Verification for Test-Time Reinforcement Learning), which introduces test-time tool verification into reward estimation. Concretely, a verifier uses an external tool as evidence (e.g., from code execution) to upweight verified rollouts in a verification-aware voting, producing more reliable pseudo-labels for training. Across various math difficulties (MATH-500, AMC, and AIME 2024) and diverse backbone types, T^3RL significantly improves over TTRL, with larger gains on harder problems. More broadly, T^3RL can be viewed as verified online data synthesis, highlighting test-time tool verification as a key mechanism for stabilizing self-evolution.
tldr: The paper introduces T^3RL, a novel test-time reinforcement learning approach that uses tool verification to improve the reliability of pseudo-labels generated through majority voting, addressing the issue of incorrect mode collapse in self-evolving large reasoning models.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了T^3RL，一种新的测试时强化学习方法，它使用工具验证来提高通过多数投票生成的伪标签的可靠性，从而解决自演化大型推理模型中不正确的模式崩溃问题。
summary_zh: 测试时强化学习（TTRL）已经成为一种很有前景的自进化大型推理模型（LRMs）范式，它能够通过多数投票产生的自诱导奖励在未标注的测试输入上进行在线适应。然而，虚假但高频的未验证共识会变成一种有偏差且被强化的奖励信号，导致错误的模式崩溃。我们使用T³RL（测试时强化学习的工具验证）来解决这种失效模式，它将测试时工具验证引入到奖励估计中。具体而言，验证器使用外部工具作为证据（例如，来自代码执行的结果）来提高验证过的rollout在验证感知投票中的权重，从而产生更可靠的伪标签用于训练。在各种数学难题（MATH-500，AMC和AIME 2024）和不同的骨干模型类型上，T³RL显著优于TTRL，并且在更难的问题上增益更大。更广泛地说，T³RL可以被视为经过验证的在线数据合成，强调了测试时工具验证是稳定自进化的关键机制。

title: Conformal Policy Control
summary: An agent must try new behaviors to explore and improve. In high-stakes environments, an agent that violates safety constraints may cause harm and must be taken offline, curtailing any future interaction. Imitating old behavior is safe, but excessive conservatism discourages exploration. How much behavior change is too much? We show how to use any safe reference policy as a probabilistic regulator for any optimized but untested policy. Conformal calibration on data from the safe policy determines how aggressively the new policy can act, while provably enforcing the user's declared risk tolerance. Unlike conservative optimization methods, we do not assume the user has identified the correct model class nor tuned any hyperparameters. Unlike previous conformal methods, our theory provides finite-sample guarantees even for non-monotonic bounded constraint functions. Our experiments on applications ranging from natural language question answering to biomolecular engineering show that safe exploration is not only possible from the first moment of deployment, but can also improve performance.
tldr: This paper introduces a conformal calibration method for safe reinforcement learning that uses a safe reference policy to regulate a new, potentially unsafe policy, providing finite-sample guarantees for constraint satisfaction without requiring model assumptions or hyperparameter tuning.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种用于安全强化学习的共形校准方法，该方法使用安全的参考策略来调节新的、可能不安全的策略，为约束满足提供有限样本保证，而无需模型假设或超参数调整。
summary_zh: 智能体必须尝试新的行为来进行探索和改进。在高风险环境中，违反安全约束的智能体可能会造成损害，必须将其离线，从而中断未来的任何交互。模仿旧行为是安全的，但过度保守会阻碍探索。多大的行为改变是过度的？我们展示了如何使用任何安全参考策略作为概率调节器来调节任何经过优化但未经测试的策略。对来自安全策略的数据进行保角校准，确定新策略可以采取多激进的行动，同时可证明地执行用户声明的风险容忍度。与保守优化方法不同，我们不假设用户已经识别出正确的模型类别或调整任何超参数。与之前的保角方法不同，我们的理论提供了有限样本保证，即使对于非单调有界约束函数也是如此。我们在从自然语言问答到生物分子工程等应用上的实验表明，安全探索不仅从部署的第一刻起就成为可能，而且还可以提高性能。

title: Specificity-aware reinforcement learning for fine-grained open-world classification
summary: Classifying fine-grained visual concepts under open-world settings, i.e., without a predefined label set, demands models to be both accurate and specific. Recent reasoning Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) exhibit strong visual understanding capability but tend to produce overly generic predictions when performing fine-grained image classification. Our preliminary analysis reveals that models do possess the intrinsic fine-grained domain knowledge. However, promoting more specific predictions (specificity) without compromising correct ones (correctness) remains a non-trivial and understudied challenge. In this work, we investigate how to steer reasoning LMMs toward predictions that are both correct and specific. We propose a novel specificity-aware reinforcement learning framework, SpeciaRL, to fine-tune reasoning LMMs on fine-grained image classification under the open-world setting. SpeciaRL introduces a dynamic, verifier-based reward signal anchored to the best predictions within online rollouts, promoting specificity while respecting the model's capabilities to prevent incorrect predictions. Our out-of-domain experiments show that SpeciaRL delivers the best trade-off between correctness and specificity across extensive fine-grained benchmarks, surpassing existing methods and advancing open-world fine-grained image classification. Code and model are publicly available at https://github.com/s-angheben/SpeciaRL.
tldr: The paper introduces SpeciaRL, a specificity-aware reinforcement learning framework, to fine-tune reasoning Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) for accurate and specific fine-grained image classification in open-world settings, showing improvements in correctness and specificity on out-of-domain benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了SpeciaRL，一个specificity-aware的强化学习框架，用于在开放世界环境中微调大型多模态模型(LMMs)，以实现准确而具体的细粒度图像分类，并在领域外基准测试中展示了其在正确性和特异性方面的改进。
summary_zh: 在开放世界设置（即没有预定义的标签集）下，对细粒度视觉概念进行分类，要求模型既精确又具体。 近期出现的推理型大型多模态模型（LMM）展现出强大的视觉理解能力，但在执行细粒度图像分类时，往往会产生过于泛化的预测。 我们的初步分析表明，模型确实具有内在的细粒度领域知识。然而，在不损害正确预测（正确性）的情况下，促进更具体的预测（特异性）仍然是一项具有挑战性且未被充分研究的问题。 在这项工作中，我们研究了如何引导推理型LMM朝着兼具正确性和特异性的预测方向发展。 我们提出了一种新颖的特异性感知强化学习框架 SpeciaRL，以便在开放世界设置下对推理型LMM 进行微调，用于细粒度图像分类。 SpeciaRL 引入了一种动态的、基于验证器的奖励信号，该信号锚定于在线推演中的最佳预测，从而在尊重模型能力以防止不正确预测的同时，提升特异性。我们的域外实验表明，SpeciaRL 在广泛的细粒度基准测试中实现了正确性和特异性之间的最佳权衡，超越了现有方法，并推动了开放世界细粒度图像分类的发展。代码和模型可在 https://github.com/s-angheben/SpeciaRL 上公开获取。

title: 3D-DRES: Detailed 3D Referring Expression Segmentation
summary: Current 3D visual grounding tasks only process sentence level detection or segmentation, which critically fails to leverage the rich compositional contextual reasonings within natural language expressions. To address this challenge, we introduce Detailed 3D Referring Expression Segmentation (3D-DRES), a new task that provides a phrase to 3D instance mapping, aiming at enhancing fine-grained 3D vision language understanding. To support 3D-DRES, we present DetailRefer, a new dataset comprising 54,432 descriptions spanning 11,054 distinct objects. Unlike previous datasets, DetailRefer implements a pioneering phrase-instance annotation paradigm where each referenced noun phrase is explicitly mapped to its corresponding 3D elements. Additionally, we introduce DetailBase, a purposefully streamlined yet effective baseline architecture that supports dual-mode segmentation at both sentence and phrase levels. Our experimental results demonstrate that models trained on DetailRefer not only excel at phrase-level segmentation but also show surprising improvements on traditional 3D-RES benchmarks.
tldr: The paper introduces a new task, 3D-DRES, and a dataset, DetailRefer, for detailed 3D referring expression segmentation at the phrase level, along with a baseline model, DetailBase, showing improvements over existing 3D-RES benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一个新的任务3D-DRES和一个数据集DetailRefer，用于短语级别的详细3D指代表达式分割，以及一个基线模型DetailBase，该模型在现有的3D-RES基准上有所改进。
summary_zh: 目前的三维视觉定位任务仅处理句子级别的检测或分割，这严重忽略了自然语言表达中丰富的组合上下文推理。为了应对这一挑战，我们引入了详细三维指代表达式分割（3D-DRES），一项新的任务，旨在提供短语到三维实例的映射，从而增强细粒度的三维视觉语言理解。为了支持3D-DRES，我们提出了DetailRefer，一个包含54,432个描述，覆盖11,054个不同对象的新数据集。与先前的数据集不同，DetailRefer采用了一种开创性的短语-实例标注范式，其中每个被引用的名词短语都被明确地映射到其对应的三维元素。此外，我们引入了DetailBase，一个经过有意简化但依旧有效的基线架构，支持句子和短语级别的双模态分割。我们的实验结果表明，在DetailRefer上训练的模型不仅在短语级别的分割方面表现出色，而且在传统的三维指代表达式分割基准测试中也表现出了令人惊讶的改进。

title: Think-as-You-See: Streaming Chain-of-Thought Reasoning for Large Vision-Language Models
summary: Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) exhibit strong Chain-of-Thought (CoT) capabilities, yet most existing paradigms assume full-video availability before inference, a batch-style process misaligned with real-world video streams where information arrives sequentially. Motivated by the streaming nature of video data, we investigate two streaming reasoning paradigms for LVLMs. The first, an interleaved paradigm, alternates between receiving frames and producing partial reasoning but remains constrained by strictly ordered cache updates. To better match streaming inputs, we propose \textbf{Think-as-You-See (TaYS)}, a unified framework enabling true concurrent reasoning. TaYS integrates parallelized CoT generation, stream-constrained training, and stream-parallel inference. It further employs temporally aligned reasoning units, streaming attention masks and positional encodings, and a dual KV-cache that decouples visual encoding from textual reasoning. We evaluate all paradigms on the Qwen2.5-VL family across representative video CoT tasks, including event dynamics analysis, causal reasoning, and thematic understanding. Experiments show that TaYS consistently outperforms both batch and interleaved baselines, improving reasoning performance while substantially reducing time-to-first-token (TTFT) and overall reasoning delay. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of data-aligned streaming reasoning in enabling efficient and responsive video understanding for LVLMs. We release our code at \href{https://github.com/EIT-NLP/StreamingLLM/tree/main/TaYS}{this repository.}
tldr: The paper introduces Think-as-You-See (TaYS), a framework for streaming chain-of-thought reasoning in LVLMs that improves performance and reduces latency in video understanding tasks by enabling truly concurrent reasoning on streaming video data.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了Think-as-You-See (TaYS)，一个用于LVLM中流式思维链推理的框架，通过在流式视频数据上实现真正的并发推理，从而提高视频理解任务的性能并减少延迟。
summary_zh: 大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）展现出强大的思维链（CoT）能力，然而，现有的大多数范式都假设在推理之前可以获取整个视频，这种批处理式流程与现实世界视频流的信息按顺序到达的特性不符。受视频数据流式性质的启发，我们研究了适用于LVLMs的两种流式推理范式。第一种是交错式范式，它在接收帧和生成部分推理之间交替进行，但仍然受到严格排序的缓存更新的约束。为了更好地匹配流式输入，我们提出了 \textbf{Think-as-You-See (TaYS)}，一个统一的框架，可以实现真正的并发推理。TaYS集成了并行化的CoT生成、流约束训练和流并行推理。它进一步采用了时间对齐的推理单元、流式注意力掩码和位置编码，以及解耦视觉编码和文本推理的双KV缓存。我们在Qwen2.5-VL系列模型上，针对具有代表性的视频CoT任务（包括事件动态分析、因果推理和主题理解）评估了所有范式。实验表明，TaYS始终优于批处理和交错式基线，在提高推理性能的同时，显著缩短了首个令牌输出时间（TTFT）和总体推理延迟。这些结果证明了数据对齐的流式推理在实现LVLMs高效且响应迅速的视频理解方面的有效性。我们在 \href{https://github.com/EIT-NLP/StreamingLLM/tree/main/TaYS}{此仓库} 发布了我们的代码。

title: VSearcher: Long-Horizon Multimodal Search Agent via Reinforcement Learning
summary: Large models are increasingly becoming autonomous agents that interact with real-world environments and use external tools to augment their static capabilities. However, most recent progress has focused on text-only large language models, which are limited to a single modality and therefore have narrower application scenarios. On the other hand, multimodal large models, while offering stronger perceptual capabilities, remain limited to static knowledge and lack the ability to access and leverage up-to-date web information. In this paper, we propose VSearcher, turning static multimodal model into multimodal search agent capable of long-horizon, multi-turn tool use in real-world web environments, including text search, image search, and web browsing, via reinforcement learning. Specifically, we introduce Iterative Injection Data Synthesis pipeline to generate large-scale, complex multimodal QA questions, which are further filtered with comprehensive metrics to ensure high quality and sufficient difficulty. We then adopt an SFT-then-RL training pipeline to turn base multimodal models to agent capable of multi-turn tool calling in real-world web environments. Besides, we propose a multimodal search benchmark MM-SearchExam dedicated to evaluating search capabilities of multimodal search agents, which proves highly challenging for recent proprietary models. Extensive evaluations across multiple multimodal search benchmarks reveal effectiveness of our method. VSearcher achieves superior performance compared to recent multimodal search agents and even surpasses several proprietary models on multimodal web search tasks.
tldr: The paper introduces VSearcher, a multimodal search agent leveraging reinforcement learning to enable multimodal large models to perform long-horizon, multi-turn tool use in real-world web environments, demonstrated on a new challenging multimodal search benchmark.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了VSearcher，一种多模态搜索代理，利用强化学习使多模态大型模型能够在真实世界的网络环境中执行长程、多轮工具使用，并在一个新的具有挑战性的多模态搜索基准上进行了演示。
summary_zh: 大型模型正日益演变为自主智能体，它们与现实世界环境交互，并利用外部工具来增强其静态能力。然而，最近的大部分进展都集中在仅文本的大型语言模型上，这些模型仅限于单一模态，因此应用场景较为狭窄。另一方面，多模态大型模型虽然提供了更强的感知能力，但仍然局限于静态知识，并且缺乏访问和利用最新网络信息的能力。在本文中，我们提出了VSearcher，将静态多模态模型转变为能够通过强化学习在真实网络环境中进行长程、多轮工具使用的多模态搜索智能体，包括文本搜索、图像搜索和网页浏览。具体来说，我们引入了迭代注入数据合成流程，以生成大规模、复杂的多模态问答问题，并通过综合指标进一步筛选这些问题，以确保高质量和足够的难度。然后，我们采用SFT-then-RL训练流程，将基础多模态模型转变为能够在真实网络环境中进行多轮工具调用的智能体。此外，我们提出了一个专门用于评估多模态搜索智能体搜索能力的多模态搜索基准MM-SearchExam，该基准对于最近的专有模型证明具有高度挑战性。在多个多模态搜索基准上的广泛评估表明了我们方法的有效性。与最近的多模态搜索智能体相比，VSearcher实现了卓越的性能，甚至在多模态网络搜索任务上超越了几种专有模型。

title: VisionCreator: A Native Visual-Generation Agentic Model with Understanding, Thinking, Planning and Creation
summary: Visual content creation tasks demand a nuanced understanding of design conventions and creative workflows-capabilities challenging for general models, while workflow-based agents lack specialized knowledge for autonomous creative planning. To overcome these challenges, we propose VisionCreator, a native visual-generation agentic model that unifies Understanding, Thinking, Planning, and Creation (UTPC) capabilities within an end-to-end learnable framework. Our work introduces four key contributions: (i) VisGenData-4k and its construction methodology using metacognition-based VisionAgent to generate high-quality creation trajectories with explicit UTPC structures; (ii) The VisionCreator agentic model, optimized through Progressive Specialization Training (PST) and Virtual Reinforcement Learning (VRL) within a high-fidelity simulated environment, enabling stable and efficient acquisition of UTPC capabilities for complex creation tasks; (iii) VisGenBench, a comprehensive benchmark featuring 1.2k test samples across diverse scenarios for standardized evaluation of multi-step visual creation capabilities; (iv) Remarkably, our VisionCreator-8B/32B models demonstrate superior performance over larger closed-source models across multiple evaluation dimensions. Overall, this work provides a foundation for future research in visual-generation agentic systems.
tldr: The paper introduces VisionCreator, a visual-generation agentic model with unified Understanding, Thinking, Planning, and Creation (UTPC) capabilities, trained using metacognition-based data generation and evaluated on a new benchmark, showing superior performance over larger closed-source models.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了VisionCreator，一个具有统一的理解、思考、计划和创造(UTPC)能力的视觉生成智能体模型。该模型使用基于元认知的生成数据进行训练，并在新的基准上进行评估，显示出优于更大的闭源模型的性能。
summary_zh: 视觉内容创作任务需要对设计规范和创意工作流程有细致的理解 —— 这对通用模型来说是具有挑战性的能力，而基于工作流程的智能体又缺乏自主创意规划的专业知识。为了克服这些挑战，我们提出了 VisionCreator，一种原生的视觉生成智能体模型，它在一个端到端可学习的框架内统一了理解、思考、计划和创造 (UTPC) 的能力。我们的工作主要有四个贡献：(i) VisGenData-4k 及其构建方法，该方法使用基于元认知的 VisionAgent 生成高质量的创作轨迹，并具有显式的 UTPC 结构；(ii) VisionCreator 智能体模型，通过渐进式专业化训练 (PST) 和虚拟强化学习 (VRL) 在高保真模拟环境中进行优化，从而能够稳定高效地获取复杂创作任务的 UTPC 能力；(iii) VisGenBench，一个全面的基准，包含 1.2k 个跨不同场景的测试样本，用于标准化评估多步骤视觉创作能力；(iv) 值得注意的是，我们的 VisionCreator-8B/32B 模型在多个评估维度上表现出优于更大规模的闭源模型的性能。总的来说，这项工作为未来视觉生成智能体系统的研究提供了基础。

title: Track4World: Feedforward World-centric Dense 3D Tracking of All Pixels
summary: Estimating the 3D trajectory of every pixel from a monocular video is crucial and promising for a comprehensive understanding of the 3D dynamics of videos. Recent monocular 3D tracking works demonstrate impressive performance, but are limited to either tracking sparse points on the first frame or a slow optimization-based framework for dense tracking. In this paper, we propose a feedforward model, called Track4World, enabling an efficient holistic 3D tracking of every pixel in the world-centric coordinate system. Built on the global 3D scene representation encoded by a VGGT-style ViT, Track4World applies a novel 3D correlation scheme to simultaneously estimate the pixel-wise 2D and 3D dense flow between arbitrary frame pairs. The estimated scene flow, along with the reconstructed 3D geometry, enables subsequent efficient 3D tracking of every pixel of this video. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that our approach consistently outperforms existing methods in 2D/3D flow estimation and 3D tracking, highlighting its robustness and scalability for real-world 4D reconstruction tasks.
tldr: The paper proposes Track4World, a feedforward model for efficient and holistic 3D tracking of every pixel in a monocular video, outperforming existing methods in 2D/3D flow estimation and 3D tracking benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了Track4World，一个前馈模型，用于对单目视频中的每个像素进行高效和整体的3D跟踪，并在2D/3D流估计和3D跟踪基准测试中优于现有方法。
summary_zh: 从单目视频估计每个像素的3D轨迹对于全面理解视频的3D动态至关重要且前景广阔。近期的单目3D跟踪工作展示了令人印象深刻的性能，但仅限于跟踪首帧上的稀疏点或用于密集跟踪的基于缓慢优化的框架。在本文中，我们提出了一种前馈模型，称为Track4World，它能够在以世界坐标系为中心的系统中对每个像素进行高效的整体3D跟踪。Track4World构建于由VGGT风格的ViT编码的全局3D场景表示之上，应用了一种新颖的3D相关方案，以同时估计任意帧对之间的像素级2D和3D密集光流。估计的场景流以及重建的3D几何体，使得能够后续高效地3D跟踪视频中的每个像素。在多个基准数据集上的大量实验表明，我们的方法在2D/3D光流估计和3D跟踪方面始终优于现有方法，突显了其在真实世界4D重建任务中的鲁棒性和可扩展性。

title: On Discriminative vs. Generative classifiers: Rethinking MLLMs for Action Understanding
summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have advanced open-world action understanding and can be adapted as generative classifiers for closed-set settings by autoregressively generating action labels as text. However, this approach is inefficient, and shared subwords across action labels introduce semantic overlap, leading to ambiguity in generation. In contrast, discriminative classifiers learn task-specific representations with clear decision boundaries, enabling efficient one-step classification without autoregressive decoding. We first compare generative and discriminative classifiers with MLLMs for closed-set action understanding, revealing the superior accuracy and efficiency of the latter. To bridge the performance gap, we design strategies that elevate generative classifiers toward performance comparable with discriminative ones. Furthermore, we show that generative modeling can complement discriminative classifiers, leading to better performance while preserving efficiency. To this end, we propose Generation-Assisted Discriminative~(GAD) classifier for closed-set action understanding. GAD operates only during fine-tuning, preserving full compatibility with MLLM pretraining. Extensive experiments on temporal action understanding benchmarks demonstrate that GAD improves both accuracy and efficiency over generative methods, achieving state-of-the-art results on four tasks across five datasets, including an average 2.5% accuracy gain and 3x faster inference on our largest COIN benchmark.
tldr: The paper compares generative and discriminative MLLMs for action understanding, proposes a Generation-Assisted Discriminative (GAD) classifier that improves accuracy and efficiency, and achieves state-of-the-art results on several benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 本文比较了生成式和判别式MLLM在动作理解方面的应用，提出了一种生成辅助判别(GAD)分类器，提高了准确率和效率，并在多个基准测试中取得了最先进的成果。
summary_zh: 多模态大型语言模型(MLLM)在开放世界动作理解方面取得了进展，并且可以通过自回归方式生成作为文本的动作标签，从而被用作闭集环境下的生成式分类器。然而，这种方法效率低下，且动作标签之间共享的子词引入了语义重叠，导致生成过程中的歧义。相比之下，判别式分类器能够学习具有清晰决策边界的特定任务表示，从而实现无需自回归解码的高效单步分类。我们首先比较了针对闭集动作理解的MLLM生成式和判别式分类器，揭示了后者在准确性和效率方面的优势。为了弥合性能差距，我们设计了策略以提升生成式分类器，使其性能与判别式分类器相当。此外，我们表明生成式建模可以补充判别式分类器，从而在保持效率的同时获得更好的性能。为此，我们提出了一种用于闭集动作理解的生成辅助判别(GAD)分类器。GAD仅在微调期间运行，完全保留了与 MLLM 预训练的兼容性。在时间动作理解基准上的大量实验表明，GAD 提高了生成式方法的准确性和效率，在五个数据集的四个任务上实现了最先进的结果，包括在我们最大的COIN基准上平均提高2.5%的准确率和3倍的更快推理速度。

title: HAMMER: Harnessing MLLM via Cross-Modal Integration for Intention-Driven 3D Affordance Grounding
summary: Humans commonly identify 3D object affordance through observed interactions in images or videos, and once formed, such knowledge can be generically generalized to novel objects. Inspired by this principle, we advocate for a novel framework that leverages emerging multimodal large language models (MLLMs) for interaction intention-driven 3D affordance grounding, namely HAMMER. Instead of generating explicit object attribute descriptions or relying on off-the-shelf 2D segmenters, we alternatively aggregate the interaction intention depicted in the image into a contact-aware embedding and guide the model to infer textual affordance labels, ensuring it thoroughly excavates object semantics and contextual cues. We further devise a hierarchical cross-modal integration mechanism to fully exploit the complementary information from the MLLM for 3D representation refinement and introduce a multi-granular geometry lifting module that infuses spatial characteristics into the extracted intention embedding, thus facilitating accurate 3D affordance localization. Extensive experiments on public datasets and our newly constructed corrupted benchmark demonstrate the superiority and robustness of HAMMER compared to existing approaches. All code and weights are publicly available.
tldr: The paper introduces HAMMER, a novel framework leveraging MLLMs for intention-driven 3D affordance grounding by aggregating interaction intention into embeddings and refining 3D representations using hierarchical cross-modal integration and multi-granular geometry lifting. Experiments show HAMMER's superiority and robustness.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为HAMMER的新框架，该框架利用多模态大型语言模型（MLLM）进行意图驱动的3D可供性基础，通过将交互意图聚合到嵌入中，并使用分层跨模态集成和多粒度几何提升来改进3D表示。实验表明HAMMER具有优越性和鲁棒性。
summary_zh: 人类通常通过观察图像或视频中的互动来识别 3D 物体的可供性，并且一旦形成，这种知识可以泛化应用到新的物体上。受此原则启发，我们提出了一种新颖的框架，利用新兴的多模态大型语言模型 (MLLM) 进行交互意图驱动的 3D 可供性定位，命名为 HAMMER。我们没有生成明确的物体属性描述或依赖现成的 2D 分割器，而是将图像中描绘的交互意图聚合到接触感知嵌入中，并引导模型推断文本可供性标签，确保其彻底挖掘物体语义和上下文线索。我们进一步设计了一种分层跨模态集成机制，以充分利用来自 MLLM 的互补信息进行 3D 表征细化，并引入一个多粒度几何提升模块，将空间特征注入到提取的意图嵌入中，从而促进精准的 3D 可供性定位。在公共数据集和我们新构建的损坏基准上的大量实验表明，与现有方法相比，HAMMER 具有优越性和鲁棒性。所有代码和权重均已公开提供。

title: Contextual Latent World Models for Offline Meta Reinforcement Learning
summary: Offline meta-reinforcement learning seeks to learn policies that generalize across related tasks from fixed datasets. Context-based methods infer a task representation from transition histories, but learning effective task representations without supervision remains a challenge. In parallel, latent world models have demonstrated strong self-supervised representation learning through temporal consistency. We introduce contextual latent world models, which condition latent world models on inferred task representations and train them jointly with the context encoder. This enforces task-conditioned temporal consistency, yielding task representations that capture task-dependent dynamics rather than merely discriminating between tasks. Our method learns more expressive task representations and significantly improves generalization to unseen tasks across MuJoCo, Contextual-DeepMind Control, and Meta-World benchmarks.
tldr: This paper introduces contextual latent world models for offline meta-RL, improving generalization to unseen tasks by learning task representations that capture task-dependent dynamics through task-conditioned temporal consistency.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种用于离线元强化学习的上下文潜在世界模型，通过学习捕获任务相关动态的任务条件时间一致性，从而提高对未见任务的泛化能力。
summary_zh: 离线元强化学习旨在从固定数据集学习可以泛化到相关任务的策略。 基于上下文的方法从转移历史中推断任务表征，但如何在没有监督的情况下学习有效的任务表征仍然是一个挑战。与此同时，潜在世界模型通过时间一致性展示了强大的自监督表征学习能力。我们引入了上下文潜在世界模型，它将潜在世界模型建立在推断的任务表征上，并与上下文编码器联合训练。 这强制执行了任务条件下的时间一致性，从而产生了能够捕获任务相关动态的任务表征，而不仅仅是区分任务。 我们的方法学习了更具表现力的任务表征，并显著提高了在 MuJoCo、Contextual-DeepMind Control 和 Meta-World 基准测试中对未见任务的泛化能力。

title: Look Forward to Walk Backward: Efficient Terrain Memory for Backward Locomotion with Forward Vision
summary: Legged robots with egocentric forward-facing depth cameras can couple exteroception and proprioception to achieve robust forward agility on complex terrain. When these robots walk backward, the forward-only field of view provides no preview. Purely proprioceptive controllers can remain stable on moderate ground when moving backward but cannot fully exploit the robot's capabilities on complex terrain and must collide with obstacles. We present Look Forward to Walk Backward (LF2WB), an efficient terrain-memory locomotion framework that uses forward egocentric depth and proprioception to write a compact associative memory during forward motion and to retrieve it for collision-free backward locomotion without rearward vision. The memory backbone employs a delta-rule selective update that softly removes then writes the memory state along the active subspace. Training uses hardware-efficient parallel computation, and deployment runs recurrent, constant-time per-step inference with a constant-size state, making the approach suitable for onboard processors on low-cost robots. Experiments in both simulations and real-world scenarios demonstrate the effectiveness of our method, improving backward agility across complex terrains under limited sensing.
tldr: The paper introduces an efficient terrain memory framework, LF2WB, enabling legged robots to walk backward safely using forward vision data captured during forward motion. This method utilizes a compact memory representation updated with a delta-rule and is demonstrated in simulations and real-world experiments.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种高效的地形记忆框架LF2WB，使腿式机器人能够利用向前运动时捕获的向前视觉数据安全地向后行走。该方法采用增量规则更新的紧凑记忆表示，并在模拟和真实场景中进行了演示。
summary_zh: 配有前向自中心深度摄像头的腿式机器人可以耦合外部感知和本体感知，从而在复杂地形上实现鲁棒的前向敏捷性。 当这些机器人向后行走时，仅有前向的视野无法提供预览信息。 纯粹的本体感知控制器在向后移动时可以在平缓地面上保持稳定，但无法在复杂地形上充分发挥机器人的性能，并且必须与障碍物发生碰撞。 我们提出“前视后走”（LF2WB），这是一种高效的地形记忆运动框架，该框架利用前向自中心深度信息和本体感知在向前运动期间编写紧凑的关联记忆，并在没有后向视觉的情况下检索该记忆，以实现无碰撞的后向运动。 该记忆骨干采用Delta规则选择性更新，以沿活动子空间柔和地移除然后写入记忆状态。 训练采用硬件高效的并行计算，部署则运行循环的、恒定时间步推理和恒定大小的状态，使得该方法适用于低成本机器人上的板载处理器。 仿真和真实场景下的实验都证明了我们方法的有效性，在有限感知条件下提高了在复杂地形上的后向敏捷性。

title: RL-Based Coverage Path Planning for Deformable Objects on 3D Surfaces
summary: Currently, manipulation tasks for deformable objects often focus on activities like folding clothes, handling ropes, and manipulating bags. However, research on contact-rich tasks involving deformable objects remains relatively underdeveloped. When humans use cloth or sponges to wipe surfaces, they rely on both vision and tactile feedback. Yet, current algorithms still face challenges with issues like occlusion, while research on tactile perception for manipulation is still evolving. Tasks such as covering surfaces with deformable objects demand not only perception but also precise robotic manipulation. To address this, we propose a method that leverages efficient and accessible simulators for task execution. Specifically, we train a reinforcement learning agent in a simulator to manipulate deformable objects for surface wiping tasks. We simplify the state representation of object surfaces using harmonic UV mapping, process contact feedback from the simulator on 2D feature maps, and use scaled grouped convolutions (SGCNN) to extract features efficiently. The agent then outputs actions in a reduced-dimensional action space to generate coverage paths. Experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms previous approaches in key metrics, including total path length and coverage area. We deploy these paths on a Kinova Gen3 manipulator to perform wiping experiments on the back of a torso model, validating the feasibility of our approach.
tldr: This paper presents a reinforcement learning (RL) approach for robotic manipulation of deformable objects to achieve surface coverage, specifically for wiping tasks, using a simulator and a simplified state representation via harmonic UV mapping and scaled grouped convolutions (SGCNN) for efficient feature extraction. The method is validated on a Kinova Gen3 robot.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种基于强化学习（RL）的方法，用于机器人操作可变形物体以实现表面覆盖，特别是擦拭任务，通过使用模拟器和简化的状态表示（通过谐波UV映射和按比例分组卷积（SGCNN）进行高效特征提取）。该方法已在 Kinova Gen3 机器人上得到验证。
summary_zh: 目前，可变形物体的操纵任务通常集中于诸如折叠衣服、处理绳索和操作袋子等活动。然而，涉及可变形物体的富接触任务的研究仍相对不发达。当人类使用布或海绵擦拭物体表面时，他们依赖于视觉和触觉反馈。然而，当前的算法仍然面临诸如遮挡的问题，而对操纵的触觉感知研究仍在发展中。用可变形物体覆盖表面等任务不仅需要感知，还需要精确的机器人操纵。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种利用高效且易于访问的模拟器来执行任务的方法。具体来说，我们在模拟器中训练强化学习智能体来操纵可变形物体进行表面擦拭任务。 我们使用调和UV映射来简化物体表面的状态表示，在2D特征图上处理来自模拟器的接触反馈，并使用缩放分组卷积 (SGCNN) 来高效地提取特征。然后，智能体在降维动作空间中输出动作以生成覆盖路径。实验表明，我们的方法在关键指标（包括总路径长度和覆盖面积）方面优于以往的方法。我们将这些路径部署在Kinova Gen3机械臂上，在躯干模型背部进行擦拭实验，验证了我们方法的可行性。

title: CASSR: Continuous A-Star Search through Reachability for real time footstep planning
summary: Footstep planning involves a challenging combinatorial search. Traditional A* approaches require discretising reachability constraints, while Mixed-Integer Programming (MIP) supports continuous formulations but quickly becomes intractable, especially when rotations are included. We present CASSR, a novel framework that recursively propagates convex, continuous formulations of a robot's kinematic constraints within an A* search. Combined with a new cost-to-go heuristic based on the EPA algorithm, CASSR efficiently plans contact sequences of up to 30 footsteps in under 125 ms. Experiments on biped locomotion tasks demonstrate that CASSR outperforms traditional discretised A* by up to a factor of 100, while also surpassing a commercial MIP solver. These results show that CASSR enables fast, reliable, and real-time footstep planning for biped robots.
tldr: The paper introduces CASSR, a novel A* search framework that uses continuous convex formulations to efficiently plan footstep sequences for bipedal robots, outperforming traditional discretized A* and MIP solvers in speed and reliability.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为CASSR的新型A*搜索框架，该框架使用连续凸公式有效地规划双足机器人的步态序列，在速度和可靠性方面优于传统的离散化A*和MIP求解器。
summary_zh: 足迹规划涉及具有挑战性的组合搜索。 传统的A*方法需要离散化可达性约束，而混合整数规划（MIP）虽然支持连续公式，但很快变得难以处理，尤其是在包含旋转时。 我们提出CASSR，一种新颖的框架，它在A*搜索中递归地传播机器人运动学约束的凸，连续公式。 结合基于EPA算法的新型cost-to-go启发式方法，CASSR能够在125毫秒内有效规划多达30个足迹步序的接触序列。 在双足运动任务的实验表明，CASSR的性能优于传统离散化的A*方法，最高可达100倍，同时也超越了商业MIP求解器。 这些结果表明，CASSR为双足机器人实现了快速、可靠和实时的足迹规划。

title: Self-supervised Domain Adaptation for Visual 3D Pose Estimation of Nano-drone Racing Gates by Enforcing Geometric Consistency
summary: We consider the task of visually estimating the relative pose of a drone racing gate in front of a nano-quadrotor, using a convolutional neural network pre-trained on simulated data to regress the gate's pose. Due to the sim-to-real gap, the pre-trained model underperforms in the real world and must be adapted to the target domain. We propose an unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) approach using only real image sequences collected by the drone flying an arbitrary trajectory in front of a gate; sequences are annotated in a self-supervised fashion with the drone's odometry as measured by its onboard sensors. On this dataset, a state consistency loss enforces that two images acquired at different times yield pose predictions that are consistent with the drone's odometry. Results indicate that our approach outperforms other SoA UDA approaches, has a low mean absolute error in position (x=26, y=28, z=10 cm) and orientation ($ψ$=13${^{\circ}}$), an improvement of 40% in position and 37% in orientation over a baseline. The approach's effectiveness is appreciable with as few as 10 minutes of real-world flight data and yields models with an inference time of 30.4ms (33 fps) when deployed aboard the Crazyflie 2.1 Brushless nano-drone.
tldr: This paper presents a self-supervised domain adaptation method for 3D pose estimation of drone racing gates. It uses geometric consistency enforced by drone odometry to bridge the sim-to-real gap, achieving improved pose estimation accuracy with minimal real-world data.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种自监督的领域自适应方法，用于无人机竞速门的三维姿态估计。它利用无人机里程计强制执行几何一致性，以弥合模拟到真实的差距，从而以最少的真实世界数据实现了改进的姿态估计精度。
summary_zh: 我们考虑这样一项任务：利用卷积神经网络，预先在模拟数据上训练，再将之用于回归模型，以视觉方式估计纳米四旋翼飞行器前方无人机竞速门的相对姿态。由于存在模拟到真实的差距，预训练模型在真实世界中的表现不佳，必须进行领域自适应。我们提出了一种无监督领域自适应（UDA）方法，该方法仅使用无人机在门前任意轨迹飞行时采集的真实图像序列；序列以自监督方式标注，使用无人机板载传感器测量的里程计数据。在该数据集上，状态一致性损失强制要求不同时间获取的两幅图像产生与无人机里程计一致的姿态预测。结果表明，我们的方法优于其他最先进的UDA方法，在位置（x=26，y=28，z=10 厘米）和方向（$ψ$=13${^{\circ}}$）上具有较低的平均绝对误差，与基线方法相比，位置误差降低了40%，方向误差降低了37%。该方法的效果在仅需10分钟的真实世界飞行数据时就非常明显，并且在部署于Crazyflie 2.1 无刷纳米无人机上时，可以产生推理时间为30.4毫秒（33 fps）的模型。

title: Tracing Back Error Sources to Explain and Mitigate Pose Estimation Failures
summary: Robust estimation of object poses in robotic manipulation is often addressed using foundational general estimators, that aim to handle diverse error sources naively within a single model. Still, they struggle due to environmental uncertainties, while requiring long inference times and heavy computation. In contrast, we propose a modular, uncertainty-aware framework that attributes pose estimation errors to specific error sources and applies targeted mitigation strategies only when necessary. Instantiated with Iterative Closest Point (ICP) as a simple and lightweight pose estimator, we leverage our framework for real-world robotic grasping tasks. By decomposing pose estimation into failure detection, error attribution, and targeted recovery, we significantly improve the robustness of ICP and achieve competitive performance compared to foundation models, while relying on a substantially simpler and faster pose estimator.
tldr: This paper introduces a modular, uncertainty-aware framework for pose estimation that attributes errors to specific sources and applies targeted mitigation strategies, achieving robustness and efficiency in robotic grasping tasks compared to general estimators.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一个模块化、不确定性感知的姿态估计框架，该框架将误差归因于特定来源并应用有针对性的缓解策略，与通用估计器相比，在机器人抓取任务中实现了鲁棒性和效率。
summary_zh: 机器人操作中目标位姿的稳健估计通常采用基础的通用估计器，其旨在在单一模型中以朴素的方式处理各种误差源。然而，此类方法由于环境的不确定性而表现不佳，同时需要较长的推理时间和高昂的计算代价。相比之下，我们提出了一种模块化的、感知不确定性的框架，该框架将位姿估计误差归因于特定的误差源，并在必要时才应用有针对性的缓解策略。以迭代最近点（ICP）作为简单且轻量级的位姿估计器为例，我们利用我们的框架进行真实的机器人抓取任务。通过将位姿估计分解为故障检测、误差归因和有针对性的恢复，我们显著提高了ICP的稳健性，并与基础模型相比实现了具有竞争力的性能，同时依赖于一个明显更简单和更快速的位姿估计器。

title: Emerging trends in Cislunar Space for Lunar Science Exploration and Space Robotics aiding Human Spaceflight Safety
summary: In recent years, the Moon has emerged as an unparalleled extraterrestrial testbed for advancing cuttingedge technological and scientific research critical to enabling sustained human presence on its surface and supporting future interplanetary exploration. This study identifies and investigates two pivotal research domains with substantial transformative potential for accelerating humanity interplanetary aspirations. First is Lunar Science Exploration with Artificial Intelligence and Space Robotics which focusses on AI and Space Robotics redefining the frontiers of space exploration. Second being Space Robotics aid in manned spaceflight to the Moon serving as critical assets for pre-deployment infrastructure development, In-Situ Resource Utilization, surface operations support, and astronaut safety assurance. By integrating autonomy, machine learning, and realtime sensor fusion, space robotics not only augment human capabilities but also serve as force multipliers in achieving sustainable lunar exploration, paving the way for future crewed missions to Mars and beyond.
tldr: This paper explores the use of AI and robotics in lunar science exploration and manned spaceflight support, focusing on enhancing human capabilities and enabling sustainable lunar exploration.
tldr_zh: 本文探讨了人工智能和机器人技术在月球科学探索和载人航天支持中的应用，重点关注增强人类能力和实现可持续月球探索。
summary_zh: 近年来，月球已成为一个独一无二的地球外试验场，用于推进前沿技术和科学研究，这对于在月球表面实现人类的长期存在并支持未来的行星际探索至关重要。本研究确定并研究了两个具有重大变革潜力的关键研究领域，以加速人类的行星际探索愿景。首先是基于人工智能和空间机器人的月球科学探索，侧重于人工智能和空间机器人重新定义太空探索的前沿。其次是空间机器人辅助载人登月航天，它们是基础设施预先部署、原位资源利用、表面作业支持和宇航员安全保障的关键资产。通过整合自主性、机器学习和实时传感器融合，空间机器人不仅增强了人类的能力，而且还成为了实现可持续月球探索的倍增器，为未来前往火星及更远区域的载人任务铺平了道路。

title: Rhythm: Learning Interactive Whole-Body Control for Dual Humanoids
summary: Realizing interactive whole-body control for multi-humanoid systems is critical for unlocking complex collaborative capabilities in shared environments. Although recent advancements have significantly enhanced the agility of individual robots, bridging the gap to physically coupled multi-humanoid interaction remains challenging, primarily due to severe kinematic mismatches and complex contact dynamics. To address this, we introduce Rhythm, the first unified framework enabling real-world deployment of dual-humanoid systems for complex, physically plausible interactions. Our framework integrates three core components: (1) an Interaction-Aware Motion Retargeting (IAMR) module that generates feasible humanoid interaction references from human data; (2) an Interaction-Guided Reinforcement Learning (IGRL) policy that masters coupled dynamics via graph-based rewards; and (3) a real-world deployment system that enables robust transfer of dual-humanoid interaction. Extensive experiments on physical Unitree G1 robots demonstrate that our framework achieves robust interactive whole-body control, successfully transferring diverse behaviors such as hugging and dancing from simulation to reality.
tldr: The paper introduces Rhythm, a framework enabling real-world deployment of dual-humanoid systems for interactive whole-body control via motion retargeting and reinforcement learning, demonstrated on Unitree G1 robots.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了Rhythm，一个通过运动重定向和强化学习实现双人形机器人交互式全身控制的框架，并在Unitree G1机器人上进行了演示。
summary_zh: 实现多人形机器人系统的交互式全身控制，对于在共享环境中开发复杂的协作能力至关重要。尽管近期的进展显著提升了单个机器人的敏捷性，但是弥合物理耦合的多人形机器人交互的差距仍然具有挑战性，这主要是由于严重的运动学不匹配和复杂的接触动力学所致。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了Rhythm，这是首个统一的框架，能够实现双人形机器人系统在现实世界中的部署，以进行复杂的、物理上可信的交互。我们的框架集成了三个核心组件：（1）一个交互感知运动重定向（IAMR）模块，该模块从人类数据中生成可行的人形机器人交互参考；（2）一个交互引导强化学习（IGRL）策略，该策略通过基于图的奖励掌握耦合动力学；（3）一个真实世界部署系统，该系统能够实现双人形机器人交互的稳健迁移。在优傲科技G1实体机器人上的大量实验表明，我们的框架实现了稳健的交互式全身控制，成功地将拥抱和跳舞等多种行为从仿真迁移到现实。

title: SPARC: Spatial-Aware Path Planning via Attentive Robot Communication
summary: Efficient communication is critical for decentralized Multi-Robot Path Planning (MRPP), yet existing learned communication methods treat all neighboring robots equally regardless of their spatial proximity, leading to diluted attention in congested regions where coordination matters most. We propose Relation enhanced Multi Head Attention (RMHA), a communication mechanism that explicitly embeds pairwise Manhattan distances into the attention weight computation, enabling each robot to dynamically prioritize messages from spatially relevant neighbors. Combined with a distance-constrained attention mask and GRU gated message fusion, RMHA integrates seamlessly with MAPPO for stable end-to-end training. In zero-shot generalization from 8 training robots to 128 test robots on 40x40 grids, RMHA achieves approximately 75 percent success rate at 30 percent obstacle density outperforming the best baseline by over 25 percentage points. Ablation studies confirm that distance-relation encoding is the key contributor to success rate improvement in high-density environments. Index Terms-Multi-robot path planning, graph attention mechanism, multi-head attention, communication optimization, cooperative decision-making
tldr: The paper introduces a spatial-aware attention mechanism (RMHA) for multi-robot path planning, improving communication efficiency and success rates in congested environments using distance-based relation encoding within a MAPPO framework.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种空间感知的注意力机制（RMHA）用于多机器人路径规划，通过在MAPPO框架内使用基于距离的关系编码，提高了拥挤环境中的通信效率和成功率。
summary_zh:
高效通信对于去中心化多机器人路径规划（MRPP）至关重要，然而，现有的学习型通信方法平等地对待所有相邻机器人，而忽略了它们的空间邻近性，导致在拥堵区域——协调最为重要的地方——注意力被稀释。我们提出了一种关系增强多头注意力（RMHA），一种将成对曼哈顿距离显式嵌入到注意力权重计算中的通信机制，从而使每个机器人能够动态地优先考虑来自空间相关邻居的消息。结合距离约束的注意力掩码和GRU门控消息融合，RMHA与MAPPO无缝集成，以实现稳定的端到端训练。在从8个训练机器人到40x40网格上128个测试机器人的零样本泛化中，在30%的障碍物密度下，RMHA实现了约75%的成功率，超过最佳基线25个百分点以上。消融研究证实，距离关系编码是提高高密度环境下成功率的关键因素。
索引词—多机器人路径规划，图注意力机制，多头注意力，通信优化，协同决策

title: Robust Tightly-Coupled Filter-Based Monocular Visual-Inertial State Estimation and Graph-Based Evaluation for Autonomous Drone Racing
summary: Autonomous drone racing (ADR) demands state estimation that is simultaneously computationally efficient and resilient to the perceptual degradation experienced during extreme velocity and maneuvers. Traditional frameworks typically rely on conventional visual-inertial pipelines with loosely-coupled gate-based Perspective-n-Points (PnP) corrections that suffer from a rigid requirement for four visible features and information loss in intermediate steps. Furthermore, the absence of GNSS and Motion Capture systems in uninstrumented, competitive racing environments makes the objective evaluation of such systems remarkably difficult. To address these limitations, we propose ADR-VINS, a robust, monocular visual-inertial state estimation framework based on an Error-State Kalman Filter (ESKF) tailored for autonomous drone racing. Our approach integrates direct pixel reprojection errors from gate corners features as innovation terms within the filter. By bypassing intermediate PnP solvers, ADR-VINS maintains valid state updates with as few as two visible corners and utilizes robust reweighting instead of RANSAC-based schemes to handle outliers, enhancing computational efficiency. Furthermore, we introduce ADR-FGO, an offline Factor-Graph Optimization framework to generate high-fidelity reference trajectories that facilitate post-flight performance evaluation and analysis on uninstrumented, GNSS-denied environments. The proposed system is validated using TII-RATM dataset, where ADR-VINS achieves an average RMS translation error of 0.134 m, while ADR-FGO yields 0.060 m as a smoothing-based reference. Finally, ADR-VINS was successfully deployed in the A2RL Drone Championship Season 2, maintaining stable and robust estimation despite noisy detections during high-agility flight at top speeds of 20.9 m/s. We further utilize ADR-FGO for post-flight evaluation in uninstrumented racing environments.
tldr: This paper introduces ADR-VINS, a robust monocular visual-inertial state estimation framework based on an Error-State Kalman Filter tailored for autonomous drone racing, and ADR-FGO, an offline Factor-Graph Optimization framework for generating high-fidelity reference trajectories for post-flight evaluation in GNSS-denied environments. It demonstrates successful deployment in a drone racing championship.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了ADR-VINS，一个基于误差状态卡尔曼滤波器的鲁棒单目视觉惯性状态估算框架，专为自主无人机竞速设计。同时提出了ADR-FGO，一个离线因子图优化框架，用于在GNSS受限环境中生成高保真参考轨迹，以便进行飞行后评估。该系统成功部署于无人机竞速锦标赛。
summary_zh: 自动无人机竞速（ADR）需要同时具备计算效率和对高速及机动飞行中感知退化具有鲁棒性的状态估计。 传统框架通常依赖于传统的视觉-惯性管道，采用松耦合的基于栅格的透视-n-点（PnP）校正，但它对四个可见特征有着严格的要求，并且在中间步骤中存在信息丢失。 此外，在无外部定位设备且具有竞争性的竞速环境中，GNSS和运动捕捉系统的缺失使得对此类系统进行客观评估变得异常困难。 为解决这些局限性，我们提出ADR-VINS，这是一种基于误差状态卡尔曼滤波器（ESKF）的稳健单目视觉-惯性状态估计框架，专门针对自动无人机竞速设计。 我们的方法将来自栅格角点特征的直接像素重投影误差作为滤波器的创新项。 通过绕过中间的PnP求解器，ADR-VINS能够以最少两个可见角点维持有效的状态更新，并利用稳健的重加权而非基于RANSAC的方案来处理外点，从而提高计算效率。 此外，我们引入ADR-FGO，这是一种离线的因子图优化框架，用于生成高保真参考轨迹，从而在无外部定位设备且GNSS拒止的环境中促进飞行后的性能评估和分析。 所提出的系统已使用TII-RATM数据集进行验证，其中ADR-VINS实现了0.134米的平均RMS平移误差，而ADR-FGO产生了0.060米作为基于平滑的参考。 最后，ADR-VINS已成功部署在A2RL无人机锦标赛第二赛季中，即使在以20.9米/秒的最高速度进行高敏捷飞行的过程中，也能保持稳定和鲁棒的估计，尽管存在嘈杂的检测。 我们进一步利用ADR-FGO在无外部定位设备的竞速环境中进行飞行后评估。

title: Improving Diffusion Planners by Self-Supervised Action Gating with Energies
summary: Diffusion planners are a strong approach for offline reinforcement learning, but they can fail when value-guided selection favours trajectories that score well yet are locally inconsistent with the environment dynamics, resulting in brittle execution. We propose Self-supervised Action Gating with Energies (SAGE), an inference-time re-ranking method that penalises dynamically inconsistent plans using a latent consistency signal. SAGE trains a Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture (JEPA) encoder on offline state sequences and an action-conditioned latent predictor for short horizon transitions. At test time, SAGE assigns each sampled candidate an energy given by its latent prediction error and combines this feasibility score with value estimates to select actions. SAGE can integrate into existing diffusion planning pipelines that can sample trajectories and select actions via value scoring; it requires no environment rollouts and no policy re-training. Across locomotion, navigation, and manipulation benchmarks, SAGE improves the performance and robustness of diffusion planners.
tldr: The paper introduces SAGE, a self-supervised method for improving diffusion-based offline reinforcement learning planners by penalizing dynamically inconsistent plans using a learned latent consistency signal, demonstrating improved performance and robustness across several benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了SAGE，一种自监督方法，通过使用学习到的潜在一致性信号惩罚动态不一致的计划，来改进基于扩散的离线强化学习规划器，并在多个基准测试中展示了改进的性能和鲁棒性。
summary_zh: 扩散规划器是离线强化学习的一种强大方法，但当价值引导的选择偏好于那些得分高但在局部上与环境动态不一致的轨迹时，它们可能会失效，导致执行的脆弱性。我们提出了一种名为“基于能量的自监督动作门控”（SAGE）的推理时重排序方法，该方法使用潜在一致性信号来惩罚动态不一致的规划。SAGE在离线状态序列上训练一个联合嵌入预测架构（JEPA）编码器，并训练一个动作条件潜在预测器用于短程转移。在测试时，SAGE为每个采样到的候选方案分配一个由其潜在预测误差给出的能量，并将此可行性评分与价值估计相结合以选择动作。SAGE可以集成到现有的扩散规划流程中，这些流程可以通过价值评分来采样轨迹和选择动作；它不需要环境模拟也不需要策略重训练。在运动、导航和操作基准测试中，SAGE提高了扩散规划器的性能和鲁棒性。

title: cuNRTO: GPU-Accelerated Nonlinear Robust Trajectory Optimization
summary: Robust trajectory optimization enables autonomous systems to operate safely under uncertainty by computing control policies that satisfy the constraints for all bounded disturbances. However, these problems often lead to large Second Order Conic Programming (SOCP) constraints, which are computationally expensive. In this work, we propose the CUDA Nonlinear Robust Trajectory Optimization (cuNRTO) framework by introducing two dynamic optimization architectures that have direct application to robust decision-making and are implemented on CUDA. The first architecture, NRTO-DR, leverages the Douglas-Rachford (DR) splitting method to solve the SOCP inner subproblems of NRTO, thereby significantly reducing the computational burden through parallel SOCP projections and sparse direct solves. The second architecture, NRTO-FullADMM, is a novel variant that further exploits the problem structure to improve scalability using the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM). Finally, we provide GPU implementation of the proposed methodologies using custom CUDA kernels for SOC projection steps and cuBLAS GEMM chains for feedback gain updates. We validate the performance of cuNRTO through simulated experiments on unicycle, quadcopter, and Franka manipulator models, demonstrating speedup up to 139.6$\times$.
tldr: The paper introduces cuNRTO, a GPU-accelerated framework for robust trajectory optimization, leveraging Douglas-Rachford splitting and ADMM to improve computational efficiency, achieving significant speedups on various robotic platforms.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了cuNRTO，一个GPU加速的鲁棒轨迹优化框架，利用Douglas-Rachford分割和ADMM来提高计算效率，并在各种机器人平台上实现了显著的加速。
summary_zh: 稳健轨迹优化通过计算满足所有有界扰动的约束的控制策略，使自主系统能够在不确定性下安全运行。然而，这些问题通常会导致大规模的二阶锥规划 (SOCP) 约束，从而造成高昂的计算成本。在这项工作中，我们提出了 CUDA 非线性稳健轨迹优化 (cuNRTO) 框架，引入了两种动态优化架构，它们可以直接应用于稳健决策，并在 CUDA 上实现。第一种架构，NRTO-DR，利用 Douglas-Rachford (DR) 分裂方法求解 NRTO 的 SOCP 内层子问题，从而通过并行 SOCP 投影和稀疏直接求解显著降低计算负担。第二种架构，NRTO-FullADMM，是一种新型变体，它进一步利用问题结构，通过交替方向乘子法 (ADMM) 提高可扩展性。最后，我们使用自定义 CUDA 内核进行 SOC 投影步骤，并使用 cuBLAS GEMM 链进行反馈增益更新，实现了所提出方法的 GPU 实现。我们通过在单轮车、四旋翼无人机和 Franka 机械臂模型上的仿真实验验证了 cuNRTO 的性能，展示了高达 139.6 倍的加速。

title: LLM-MLFFN: Multi-Level Autonomous Driving Behavior Feature Fusion via Large Language Model
summary: Accurate classification of autonomous vehicle (AV) driving behaviors is critical for safety validation, performance diagnosis, and traffic integration analysis. However, existing approaches primarily rely on numerical time-series modeling and often lack semantic abstraction, limiting interpretability and robustness in complex traffic environments. This paper presents LLM-MLFFN, a novel large language model (LLM)-enhanced multi-level feature fusion network designed to address the complexities of multi-dimensional driving data. The proposed LLM-MLFFN framework integrates priors from largescale pre-trained models and employs a multi-level approach to enhance classification accuracy. LLM-MLFFN comprises three core components: (1) a multi-level feature extraction module that extracts statistical, behavioral, and dynamic features to capture the quantitative aspects of driving behaviors; (2) a semantic description module that leverages LLMs to transform raw data into high-level semantic features; and (3) a dual-channel multi-level feature fusion network that combines numerical and semantic features using weighted attention mechanisms to improve robustness and prediction accuracy. Evaluation on the Waymo open trajectory dataset demonstrates the superior performance of the proposed LLM-MLFFN, achieving a classification accuracy of over 94%, surpassing existing machine learning models. Ablation studies further validate the critical contributions of multi-level fusion, feature extraction strategies, and LLM-derived semantic reasoning. These results suggest that integrating structured feature modeling with language-driven semantic abstraction provides a principled and interpretable pathway for robust autonomous driving behavior classification.
tldr: This paper introduces LLM-MLFFN, a novel LLM-enhanced multi-level feature fusion network for autonomous vehicle driving behavior classification, demonstrating superior accuracy on the Waymo Open Dataset.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为LLM-MLFFN的新型LLM增强型多级特征融合网络，用于自动驾驶车辆驾驶行为分类，并在Waymo开放数据集上表现出卓越的准确性。
summary_zh: 自动驾驶车辆（AV）驾驶行为的准确分类对于安全验证、性能诊断和交通融合分析至关重要。然而，现有的方法主要依赖于数值时间序列建模，并且通常缺乏语义抽象，从而限制了在复杂交通环境中的可解释性和鲁棒性。本文提出了一种新颖的基于大型语言模型（LLM）的多级特征融合网络LLM-MLFFN，旨在解决多维驾驶数据的复杂性。所提出的LLM-MLFFN框架集成了来自大规模预训练模型的先验知识，并采用多级方法来提高分类精度。LLM-MLFFN包括三个核心组件：（1）多级特征提取模块，用于提取统计、行为和动态特征，以捕捉驾驶行为的定量方面；（2）语义描述模块，利用LLM将原始数据转换为高层次的语义特征；（3）双通道多级特征融合网络，使用加权注意力机制结合数值和语义特征，以提高鲁棒性和预测精度。在Waymo开放轨迹数据集上的评估表明，所提出的LLM-MLFFN具有卓越的性能，实现了超过94%的分类精度，超过了现有的机器学习模型。消融实验进一步验证了多级融合、特征提取策略和LLM派生的语义推理的关键贡献。这些结果表明，将结构化特征建模与语言驱动的语义抽象相结合，为鲁棒的自动驾驶行为分类提供了一条有原则且可解释的途径。

title: Strategic Shaping of Human Prosociality: A Latent-State POMDP Framework
summary: We propose a decision-theoretic framework in which a robot strategically can shape inferred human's prosocial state during repeated interactions. Modeling the human's prosociality as a latent state that evolves over time, the robot learns to infer and influence this state through its own actions, including helping and signaling. We formalize this as a latent-state POMDP with limited observations and learn the transition and observation dynamics using expectation maximization. The resulting belief-based policy balances task and social objectives, selecting actions that maximize long-term cooperative outcomes. We evaluate the model using data from user studies and show that the learned policy outperforms baseline strategies in both team performance and increasing observed human cooperative behavior.
tldr: This paper proposes a POMDP-based framework for robots to strategically shape human prosocial behavior during repeated interactions, demonstrating improved team performance and increased human cooperation through learned policies.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种基于POMDP框架，用于机器人策略性地塑造重复交互中人类亲社会行为的方法。通过学习策略，该方法展示了改进的团队表现和增加的人类合作行为。
summary_zh: 我们提出一个基于决策理论的框架，在该框架中，机器人在重复交互过程中可以策略性地塑造人类推断出的亲社会状态。将人类的亲社会性建模为一个随时间演变的潜在状态，机器人通过自身的行动，包括帮助和发出信号，来学习推断和影响这个状态。我们将其形式化为一个具有有限观察值的潜在状态POMDP，并使用期望最大化算法学习其转移和观察动力学。由此产生的基于信念的策略平衡了任务和社会目标，选择最大化长期合作成果的行动。我们使用来自用户研究的数据评估了该模型，并表明所学习到的策略在团队表现和增加观察到的人类合作行为方面均优于基线策略。

title: Reinforcement Learning with Symbolic Reward Machines
summary: Reward Machines (RMs) are an established mechanism in Reinforcement Learning (RL) to represent and learn sparse, temporally extended tasks with non-Markovian rewards. RMs rely on high-level information in the form of labels that are emitted by the environment alongside the observation. However, this concept requires manual user input for each environment and task. The user has to create a suitable labeling function that computes the labels. These limitations lead to poor applicability in widely adopted RL frameworks. We propose Symbolic Reward Machines (SRMs) together with the learning algorithms QSRM and LSRM to overcome the limitations of RMs. SRMs consume only the standard output of the environment and process the observation directly through guards that are represented by symbolic formulas. In our evaluation, our SRM methods outperform the baseline RL approaches and generate the same results as the existing RM methods. At the same time, our methods adhere to the widely used environment definition and provide interpretable representations of the task to the user.
tldr: The paper proposes Symbolic Reward Machines (SRMs) to address the manual labeling requirement of standard Reward Machines in Reinforcement Learning, enabling direct processing of environment observations via symbolic formulas. SRMs achieve comparable or improved performance while adhering to widely used RL environments.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了符号奖励机(SRMs)，以解决强化学习中现有奖励机对手动标签的需求。SRMs可以直接通过符号公式处理环境观察，并在广泛使用的RL环境中实现可比或更优的性能。
summary_zh: 奖励机（RMs）是强化学习（RL）中一种成熟的机制，用于表示和学习具有非马尔可夫奖励的稀疏、时间扩展任务。 奖励机依赖于由环境和观察一同产生的高级标签信息。 然而，这个概念需要针对每个环境和任务进行手动用户输入。 用户必须创建一个合适的标签函数来计算标签。 这些限制导致其在广泛采用的RL框架中的适用性较差。 我们提出了符号奖励机（SRMs），以及学习算法QSRM和LSRM，以克服RM的局限性。 SRM仅使用环境的标准输出，并通过由符号公式表示的守卫直接处理观察。 在我们的评估中，我们的SRM方法优于基线RL方法，并生成与现有RM方法相同的结果。 同时，我们的方法遵循广泛使用的环境定义，并向用户提供任务的可解释表示。

title: Guideline-Grounded Evidence Accumulation for High-Stakes Agent Verification
summary: As LLM-powered agents have been used for high-stakes decision-making, such as clinical diagnosis, it becomes critical to develop reliable verification of their decisions to facilitate trustworthy deployment. Yet, existing verifiers usually underperform owing to a lack of domain knowledge and limited calibration. To address this, we establish GLEAN, an agent verification framework with Guideline-grounded Evidence Accumulation that compiles expert-curated protocols into trajectory-informed, well-calibrated correctness signals. GLEAN evaluates the step-wise alignment with domain guidelines and aggregates multi-guideline ratings into surrogate features, which are accumulated along the trajectory and calibrated into correctness probabilities using Bayesian logistic regression. Moreover, the estimated uncertainty triggers active verification, which selectively collects additional evidence for uncertain cases via expanding guideline coverage and performing differential checks. We empirically validate GLEAN with agentic clinical diagnosis across three diseases from the MIMIC-IV dataset, surpassing the best baseline by 12% in AUROC and 50% in Brier score reduction, which confirms the effectiveness in both discrimination and calibration. In addition, the expert study with clinicians recognizes GLEAN's utility in practice.
tldr: The paper introduces GLEAN, a framework for verifying LLM-powered agent decisions in high-stakes scenarios like clinical diagnosis, using guideline-grounded evidence accumulation and active verification to improve accuracy and calibration.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了GLEAN，一个用于验证LLM驱动的智能体在高风险场景（如临床诊断）中的决策的框架，它利用基于指南的证据积累和主动验证来提高准确率和校准。
summary_zh:
摘要：
随着由大型语言模型驱动的智能体被应用于高风险决策制定，如临床诊断，开发对其决策的可靠验证机制以促进可信赖部署变得至关重要。然而，现有的验证器通常表现不佳，原因是缺乏领域知识和校准不足。为了解决这个问题，我们构建了 GLEAN，一个基于指南理论证据累积的智能体验证框架，它将专家策划的协议编译成trajectory-informed、良好校准的正确性信号。GLEAN评估智能体与领域指南的逐步对齐，并将多指南评分聚合为代理特征，这些特征沿轨迹累积，并使用贝叶斯逻辑回归校准为正确性概率。此外，估计的不确定性会触发主动验证，通过扩展指南覆盖范围和执行差异检查，为不确定的案例选择性地收集额外证据。我们通过MIMIC-IV数据集中的三种疾病的智能体临床诊断，对GLEAN进行了实证验证，在AUROC上超越了最佳基线12%，并在Brier分数降低上超越了50%，这证实了其在区分能力和校准方面的有效性。此外，与临床医生的专家研究也认可了GLEAN在实践中的实用性。

title: iGVLM: Dynamic Instruction-Guided Vision Encoding for Question-Aware Multimodal Understanding
summary: Despite the success of Large Vision--Language Models (LVLMs), most existing architectures suffer from a representation bottleneck: they rely on static, instruction-agnostic vision encoders whose visual representations are utilized in an invariant manner across different textual tasks. This rigidity hinders fine-grained reasoning where task-specific visual cues are critical. To address this issue, we propose iGVLM, a general framework for instruction-guided visual modulation. iGVLM introduces a decoupled dual-branch architecture: a frozen representation branch that preserves task-agnostic visual representations learned during pre-training, and a dynamic conditioning branch that performs affine feature modulation via Adaptive Layer Normalization (AdaLN). This design enables a smooth transition from general-purpose perception to instruction-aware reasoning while maintaining the structural integrity and stability of pre-trained visual priors. Beyond standard benchmarks, we introduce MM4, a controlled diagnostic probe for quantifying logical consistency under multi-query, multi-instruction settings. Extensive results show that iGVLM consistently enhances instruction sensitivity across diverse language backbones, offering a plug-and-play paradigm for bridging passive perception and active reasoning.
tldr: The paper introduces iGVLM, a method for enhancing Vision-Language Models by making the visual encoding process instruction-aware through a decoupled dual-branch architecture and adaptive layer normalization.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了iGVLM，一种通过解耦的双分支架构和自适应层归一化，使视觉编码过程具有指令意识，从而增强视觉-语言模型的方法。
summary_zh:
摘要：
尽管大型视觉-语言模型（LVLMs）取得了成功，但现有的大多数架构都存在表示瓶颈：它们依赖于静态的、与指令无关的视觉编码器，其视觉表示以一种不变的方式应用于不同的文本任务。这种刚性阻碍了需要特定任务视觉线索的细粒度推理。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了iGVLM，一个用于指令引导视觉调制的通用框架。iGVLM引入了一种解耦的双分支架构：一个冻结的表示分支，用于保留预训练期间学习到的与任务无关的视觉表示；以及一个动态的调节分支，通过自适应层归一化（AdaLN）执行仿射特征调制。这种设计能够实现从通用感知到指令感知推理的平滑过渡，同时保持预训练视觉先验的结构完整性和稳定性。除了标准基准之外，我们还引入了MM4，一种受控的诊断探针，用于量化多查询、多指令设置下的逻辑一致性。大量结果表明，iGVLM持续增强了不同语言主干模型的指令敏感性，为连接被动感知和主动推理提供了一种即插即用的范例。

title: Diagnosing Retrieval vs. Utilization Bottlenecks in LLM Agent Memory
summary: Memory-augmented LLM agents store and retrieve information from prior interactions, yet the relative importance of how memories are written versus how they are retrieved remains unclear. We introduce a diagnostic framework that analyzes how performance differences manifest across write strategies, retrieval methods, and memory utilization behavior, and apply it to a 3x3 study crossing three write strategies (raw chunks, Mem0-style fact extraction, MemGPT-style summarization) with three retrieval methods (cosine, BM25, hybrid reranking). On LoCoMo, retrieval method is the dominant factor: average accuracy spans 20 points across retrieval methods (57.1% to 77.2%) but only 3-8 points across write strategies. Raw chunked storage, which requires zero LLM calls, matches or outperforms expensive lossy alternatives, suggesting that current memory pipelines may discard useful context that downstream retrieval mechanisms fail to compensate for. Failure analysis shows that performance breakdowns most often manifest at the retrieval stage rather than at utilization. We argue that, under current retrieval practices, improving retrieval quality yields larger gains than increasing write-time sophistication. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/boqiny/memory-probe.
tldr: This paper investigates the bottlenecks in LLM agent memory, finding retrieval methods to be a more significant factor than write strategies, suggesting improvements to retrieval quality are more impactful than enhancing write-time sophistication.
tldr_zh: 该论文研究了LLM智能体记忆中的瓶颈，发现检索方法比写入策略更重要，表明改进检索质量比提高写入时的复杂性更有效。
summary_zh: 记忆增强型LLM智能体存储并检索来自先前交互的信息，然而记忆写入与检索方式的相对重要性仍然不明确。我们引入了一种诊断框架，用于分析性能差异如何在写入策略、检索方法和记忆利用行为中体现出来，并将其应用于一个3x3的研究，该研究交叉考察了三种写入策略（原始块、Mem0式的事实提取、MemGPT式的摘要）和三种检索方法（余弦相似度、BM25、混合重排序）。在 LoCoMo 上，检索方法是主要因素：平均准确率在检索方法之间相差 20 个百分点（57.1% 到 77.2%），但在写入策略之间仅相差 3-8 个百分点。原始分块存储，即不需要任何LLM调用，可以匹配甚至优于代价高昂的有损替代方案，这表明当前的记忆管道可能会丢弃下游检索机制无法弥补的有用的上下文信息。失效分析表明，性能下降最常发生在检索阶段，而不是利用阶段。我们认为，在当前的检索实践下，提高检索质量比提高写入时的复杂性产生更大的收益。代码已公开，地址为 https://github.com/boqiny/memory-probe。

title: PlayWrite: A Multimodal System for AI Supported Narrative Co-Authoring Through Play in XR
summary: Current AI writing tools, which rely on text prompts, poorly support the spatial and interactive nature of storytelling where ideas emerge from direct manipulation and play. We present PlayWrite, a mixed-reality system where users author stories by directly manipulating virtual characters and props. A multi-agent AI pipeline interprets these actions into Intent Frames -structured narrative beats visualized as rearrangeable story marbles on a timeline. A large language model then transforms the user's assembled sequence into a final narrative. A user study (N=13) with writers from varying domains found that PlayWrite fosters a highly improvisational and playful process. Users treated the AI as a collaborative partner, using its unexpected responses to spark new ideas and overcome creative blocks. PlayWrite demonstrates an approach for co-creative systems that move beyond text to embrace direct manipulation and play as core interaction modalities.
tldr: PlayWrite is a mixed-reality system for AI-supported narrative co-authoring where users manipulate virtual elements to create stories, which are then translated into narrative beats by a multi-agent AI pipeline and finalized by a large language model. User studies suggest it fosters a playful and collaborative writing process.
tldr_zh: PlayWrite是一个混合现实系统，用于人工智能辅助的叙事共同创作，用户通过操纵虚拟元素来创建故事，这些故事随后被多智能体AI管道转换为叙事节拍，并由大型语言模型最终完成。用户研究表明，它促进了一个有趣且协作的写作过程。
summary_zh: 当前的人工智能写作工具依赖文本提示，在故事创作中对空间性和互动性支持不足，而空间性和互动性是创意来源于直接操作和玩耍的地方。我们提出PlayWrite，一个混合现实系统，用户可以通过直接操纵虚拟角色和道具创作故事。一个多智能体AI流水线将这些动作解读为意图帧——结构化的叙事节拍，并将其可视化为时间轴上可重新排列的故事弹珠。然后，一个大型语言模型将用户组装的序列转换为最终的叙事。一项针对来自不同领域的写作者的用户研究（N=13）发现，PlayWrite促进了一个高度即兴和活泼的过程。用户将AI视为合作伙伴，利用其意想不到的反应来激发新的想法并克服创作障碍。PlayWrite展示了一种协同创作系统的方法，该系统超越文本，将直接操作和玩耍作为核心交互方式。

title: Adaptive Confidence Regularization for Multimodal Failure Detection
summary: The deployment of multimodal models in high-stakes domains, such as self-driving vehicles and medical diagnostics, demands not only strong predictive performance but also reliable mechanisms for detecting failures. In this work, we address the largely unexplored problem of failure detection in multimodal contexts. We propose Adaptive Confidence Regularization (ACR), a novel framework specifically designed to detect multimodal failures. Our approach is driven by a key observation: in most failure cases, the confidence of the multimodal prediction is significantly lower than that of at least one unimodal branch, a phenomenon we term confidence degradation. To mitigate this, we introduce an Adaptive Confidence Loss that penalizes such degradations during training. In addition, we propose Multimodal Feature Swapping, a novel outlier synthesis technique that generates challenging, failure-aware training examples. By training with these synthetic failures, ACR learns to more effectively recognize and reject uncertain predictions, thereby improving overall reliability. Extensive experiments across four datasets, three modalities, and multiple evaluation settings demonstrate that ACR achieves consistent and robust gains. The source code will be available at https://github.com/mona4399/ACR.
tldr: This paper introduces Adaptive Confidence Regularization (ACR) for detecting failures in multimodal models. ACR uses an adaptive confidence loss and multimodal feature swapping to improve the reliability of predictions in high-stakes domains.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了用于检测多模态模型故障的自适应置信度正则化（ACR）。ACR使用自适应置信度损失和多模态特征交换来提高在高风险领域中预测的可靠性。
summary_zh: 在自动驾驶汽车和医学诊断等高风险领域部署多模态模型，不仅要求强大的预测性能，还要求可靠的故障检测机制。 本文旨在解决多模态环境中鲜为人知的故障检测问题。 我们提出了一种专门用于检测多模态故障的新型框架——自适应置信度正则化 (ACR)。 我们的方法基于一个关键观察：在大多数故障情况下，多模态预测的置信度明显低于至少一个单模态分支的置信度，我们称之为置信度退化。 为了缓解这种情况，我们引入了一种自适应置信度损失，以惩罚训练期间的此类退化。 此外，我们提出了一种新颖的异常值合成技术——多模态特征交换，用于生成具有挑战性的、具备故障意识的训练样本。 通过使用这些合成故障进行训练，ACR 能够更有效地识别和拒绝不确定的预测，从而提高整体可靠性。 在四个数据集、三种模态和多个评估设置下进行的大量实验表明，ACR 实现了持续且稳健的提升。 源代码将在 https://github.com/mona4399/ACR 上提供。

title: Sketch2Colab: Sketch-Conditioned Multi-Human Animation via Controllable Flow Distillation
summary: We present Sketch2Colab, which turns storyboard-style 2D sketches into coherent, object-aware 3D multi-human motion with fine-grained control over agents, joints, timing, and contacts. Conventional diffusion-based motion generators have advanced realism; however, achieving precise adherence to rich interaction constraints typically demands extensive training and/or costly posterior guidance, and performance can degrade under strong multi-entity conditioning. Sketch2Colab instead first learns a sketch-driven diffusion prior and then distills it into an efficient rectified-flow student operating in latent space for fast, stable sampling. Differentiable energies over keyframes, trajectories, and physics-based constraints directly shape the student's transport field, steering samples toward motions that faithfully satisfy the storyboard while remaining physically plausible. To capture coordinated interaction, we augment the continuous flow with a continuous-time Markov chain (CTMC) planner that schedules discrete events such as touches, grasps, and handoffs, modulating the dynamics to produce crisp, well-phased human-object-human collaborations. Experiments on CORE4D and InterHuman show that Sketch2Colab achieves state-of-the-art constraint adherence and perceptual quality while offering significantly faster inference than diffusion-only baselines.
tldr: Sketch2Colab generates realistic and controllable multi-human animations from storyboard sketches by distilling a diffusion prior into a fast rectified-flow network and incorporating a CTMC-based interaction planner, achieving state-of-the-art constraint adherence and speed.
tldr_zh: Sketch2Colab 通过将扩散先验提炼成快速的修正 flow 网络并结合基于 CTMC 的交互规划器，从故事板草图生成逼真且可控的多人动画，从而实现了最先进的约束遵循和速度。
summary_zh: 我们提出了 Sketch2Colab，它将故事板风格的 2D 草图转化为连贯的、对象感知的 3D 多人运动，并对代理、关节、时间安排和接触进行细粒度控制。传统的基于扩散的运动生成器在真实感方面取得了进展；然而，要精确地遵守丰富的交互约束，通常需要大量的训练和/或昂贵的后验指导，并且在强多实体条件下的性能可能会下降。Sketch2Colab 首先学习素描驱动的扩散先验，然后将其提炼成在潜在空间中运行的高效纠正流学生模型，以实现快速、稳定的采样。关键帧、轨迹和基于物理的约束上的可微能量直接塑造了学生模型的传输场，引导样本朝着忠实地满足故事板要求且在物理上合理的运动方向发展。为了捕捉协调的交互，我们使用连续时间马尔可夫链 (CTMC) 规划器来增强连续流，该规划器调度诸如触摸、抓握和传递等离散事件，从而调节动力学以产生清晰、良好相位的人-物-人协作。在 CORE4D 和 InterHuman 上的实验表明，Sketch2Colab 在约束遵守和感知质量方面实现了最先进的性能，同时提供了比仅使用扩散的基线方法快得多的推理速度。

title: Boltzmann-based Exploration for Robust Decentralized Multi-Agent Planning
summary: Decentralized Monte Carlo Tree Search (Dec-MCTS) is widely used for cooperative multi-agent planning but struggles in sparse or skewed reward environments. We introduce Coordinated Boltzmann MCTS (CB-MCTS), which replaces deterministic UCT with a stochastic Boltzmann policy and a decaying entropy bonus for sustained yet focused exploration. While Boltzmann exploration has been studied in single-agent MCTS, applying it in multi-agent systems poses unique challenges. CB-MCTS is the first to address this. We analyze CB-MCTS in the simple-regret setting and show in simulations that it outperforms Dec-MCTS in deceptive scenarios and remains competitive on standard benchmarks, providing a robust solution for multi-agent planning.
tldr: The paper introduces Coordinated Boltzmann MCTS (CB-MCTS), a novel extension of Dec-MCTS using Boltzmann exploration to improve robustness in sparse reward multi-agent planning scenarios, demonstrating superior performance in deceptive environments.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了协调玻尔兹曼蒙特卡洛树搜索(CB-MCTS)，这是一种Dec-MCTS的新扩展，它使用玻尔兹曼探索来提高稀疏奖励多智能体规划场景中的鲁棒性，并在欺骗性环境中表现出更好的性能。
summary_zh: 分散式蒙特卡洛树搜索（Dec-MCTS）被广泛应用于合作多智能体规划，但在稀疏或偏斜奖励环境中表现不佳。我们引入了协调玻尔兹曼MCTS（CB-MCTS），它用随机玻尔兹曼策略和衰减的熵奖励替代了确定性的UCT，以实现持续且集中的探索。虽然玻尔兹曼探索已在单智能体MCTS中得到研究，但将其应用于多智能体系统带来了独特的挑战。CB-MCTS是首个解决该挑战的方法。我们在简单遗憾设置下分析了CB-MCTS，并在仿真中表明其在欺骗性场景中优于Dec-MCTS，并在标准基准测试中保持了竞争力，为多智能体规划提供了一种稳健的解决方案。

title: CGL: Advancing Continual GUI Learning via Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
summary: Graphical User Interface (GUI) Agents, benefiting from recent advances in multimodal large language models (MLLM), have achieved significant development. However, due to the frequent updates of GUI applications, adapting to new tasks without forgetting old tasks in GUI continual learning remains an open problem. In this work, we reveal that while Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) facilitates fast adaptation, it often triggers knowledge overwriting, whereas Reinforcement Learning (RL) demonstrates an inherent resilience that shields prior interaction logic from erasure. Based on this insight, we propose a \textbf{C}ontinual \textbf{G}UI \textbf{L}earning (CGL) framework that dynamically balances adaptation efficiency and skill retention by enhancing the synergy between SFT and RL. Specifically, we introduce an SFT proportion adjustment mechanism guided by policy entropy to dynamically control the weight allocation between the SFT and RL training phases. To resolve explicit gradient interference, we further develop a specialized gradient surgery strategy. By projecting exploratory SFT gradients onto GRPO-based anchor gradients, our method explicitly clips the components of SFT gradients that conflict with GRPO. On top of that, we establish an AndroidControl-CL benchmark, which divides GUI applications into distinct task groups to effectively simulate and evaluate the performance of continual GUI learning. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed CGL framework across continual learning scenarios. The benchmark, code, and model will be made publicly available.
tldr: The paper presents a continual learning framework (CGL) for GUI agents that leverages both SFT and RL, dynamically balancing adaptation and skill retention using policy entropy and gradient surgery. They also introduce a new AndroidControl-CL benchmark.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一个用于GUI代理的持续学习框架（CGL），该框架利用SFT和RL，使用策略熵和梯度手术动态平衡适应性和技能保留。他们还引入了一个新的AndroidControl-CL基准测试。
summary_zh: 图形用户界面（GUI）智能体，受益于多模态大型语言模型（MLLM）的最新进展，已经取得了显著的发展。然而，由于GUI应用程序的频繁更新，在GUI持续学习中适应新任务而不遗忘旧任务仍然是一个开放性问题。在这项工作中，我们揭示了监督式微调（SFT）虽然有助于快速适应，但它常常触发知识覆盖，而强化学习（RL）则表现出一种固有的弹性，可以保护先前的交互逻辑免受擦除。基于这一洞察，我们提出了一个\textbf{C}ontinual \textbf{G}UI \textbf{L}earning（CGL）框架，该框架通过增强SFT和RL之间的协同作用来动态平衡适应效率和技能保留。具体而言，我们引入了一种由策略熵引导的SFT比例调整机制，以动态控制SFT和RL训练阶段之间的权重分配。为了解决显式的梯度干扰，我们进一步开发了一种专门的梯度手术策略。通过将探索性的SFT梯度投影到基于GRPO的锚定梯度上，我们的方法显式地裁剪了与GRPO冲突的SFT梯度的分量。最重要的是，我们建立了一个AndroidControl-CL基准，该基准将GUI应用程序划分为不同的任务组，以有效地模拟和评估持续GUI学习的性能。实验结果表明了我们提出的CGL框架在持续学习场景中的有效性。该基准、代码和模型将公开提供。

title: Articulation in Motion: Prior-free Part Mobility Analysis for Articulated Objects By Dynamic-Static Disentanglement
summary: Articulated objects are ubiquitous in daily life. Our goal is to achieve a high-quality reconstruction, segmentation of independent moving parts, and analysis of articulation. Recent methods analyse two different articulation states and perform per-point part segmentation, optimising per-part articulation using cross-state correspondences, given a priori knowledge of the number of parts. Such assumptions greatly limit their applications and performance. Their robustness is reduced when objects cannot be clearly visible in both states. To address these issues, in this paper, we present a new framework, Articulation in Motion (AiM).
